all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 819 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +43% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Orb Of Growth (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 5,966 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Brawler
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 24 — about 3,689 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +40% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Spectral Schematics over Megacosm (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 36% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 2,705 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +35% more 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Spirit Of Amir (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Kallari's main threat is Guillotine; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Oblivion Crown: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite, at the cost of 24% of this kit's damage.
